hero.label: About
hero.sub: Most digital health doesn't fall short because of bad technology. It falls short because it is built against the wrong definition of value, and I set up my practice to fix that.

founded.label: Why I exist

title.pre: Real clinical value doesn't have to be

paras: People building and implementing digital health know that clinical outcomes matter. But those outcomes can feel far off, and hard to move directly.

So things drift. The commercial narrative pulls one way, the product roadmap another, and the evidence lands somewhere else entirely. Clinical value gets lost in translation, and teams move fast, just not towards what actually matters for people.
When users and buyers see bold claims without the evidence to back them, trust wanes. Clients churn. Funding dries up.
After a decade inside venture-backed digital health companies and global institutions, I set up my practice to change that: to bring sharp focus back to clinical outcomes, and to what really matters.

items.text: NHS primary and urgent care, UK
Global health and primary care strengthening, Sierra Leone
Former Medical Director at Ada Health, with clinical leadership advisory for some of Europe's biggest health tech companies.
Built a regulatory-compliant medical quality function covering model evaluation, post-deployment monitoring, and clinical evaluation.
Led cross-functional teams putting regulated clinical AI into health systems across the US, Europe, and Africa, earning lasting trust with clinical leaders through demonstrable outcomes.
Commercial, product, and clinical leadership that sharpened go-to-market and evidence generation, with narratives that won and retained multi-million-dollar partnerships.

cards.title: Shaping policy and the last mile of care

items.text: Former Co-chair, Clinical Evaluation Group, WHO initiative on AI for Health, developing global guidelines for evaluating AI in healthcare.
Keynotes and talks at HLTH, HIMSS, WHO, the Wellcome Trust, Doctolib, and the Global Digital Health Forum.
Guest Editor, Nature npj Digital Medicine, special collection on implementation in low-resource settings.

cta1.text: Think we might be a fit? I'd love to hear from you.

person.imageAlt: Dr Shubs Upadhyay speaking on stage
person.label: The person behind it
person.name: Dr Shubs Upadhyay
paras: I'm a physician and digital health strategist with fifteen years across clinical practice, health technology, and health system strategy, from frontline care in the NHS, Australia, and Sierra Leone to medical leadership inside venture-backed health tech.

As former Medical Director at Ada Health and co-chair of the WHO Clinical Evaluation Group for AI in healthcare, I've sat on both the clinical and the commercial side of the table. I set up independently to give digital health organisations the kind of senior clinical and strategic counsel they rarely have access to.

intro.text: about digital health strategy, evidence, and equity.

manifesto.label: How I work
manifesto.pre: Digital health must be built around clinical reality, not around what is easy to build or easy to
points: I don't accept that moving fast means cutting clinical corners.
I don't accept that evidence is just a box to tick for regulators.
I don't accept that doing right by patients and growing the business are competing goals.
manifesto.close: The organisations that understand this are the ones that last.

cta.label: Let's work together
cta.body: Every engagement starts by understanding what you are actually trying to achieve. No pitch, no hard sell. Just an honest conversation.
